rule: Start with outcome, not repository state.
why: Repo state is evidence, not the product decision.

rule: Keep one active bet visible and sequence everything else.
why: Reduces context switching and makes the next few hours obvious.

rule: Group projects by decision lane: build, decide, consolidate, park.
why: Portfolio state should answer what kind of attention each project needs.

rule: Act as a development partner: surface choices, risks, and next actions.
why: The LLM should help steer the work, not only report facts.

rule: Show only the first useful reminder in the board; keep deeper notes in structured data.
why: Phone view needs signal before completeness.

rule: When the panel feels chaotic, remove categories before adding more UI.
why: Simpler mental models beat denser dashboards.

rule: Public surfaces should ladder up to one product story.
why: too.foo, e51, portfolio, and Kaggle should not fragment the vision.

rule: Machine rebuild is a project with outcomes, risks, and sequencing.
why: Cleanup work should not appear as random chores.

rule: Vendor/lab work should be parked unless it becomes a deliberate product bet.
why: Dependencies and experiments should not compete with active platform work.
